issue_comments.body:
## Summary of Changes Hello, I'm Gemini Code Assist[^1]! I'm currently reviewing this pull request and will post my feedback shortly. In the meantime, here's a summary to help you and other reviewers quickly get up to speed! This pull request significantly improves the robustness and consistency of the database migration system. It addresses several critical discrepancies between the SQLAlchemy models and the actual database schema, ensuring that 'alembic autogenerate' accurately reflects changes and that migrations function correctly across both SQLite and PostgreSQL environments. Key changes include enhancing model visibility for Alembic, correcting schema definitions for core tables, and implementing dialect-aware, idempotent migration patterns to prevent future issues. A new migration auditor agent and hook were also introduced to proactively guide developers in maintaining migration integrity. ### Highlights * **Alembic Migration Auditor Agent and Hook**: Introduced an Alembic migration auditor agent and a PostToolUse hook to automatically suggest migration audits when SQLAlchemy models or migration files are modified, enhancing proactive detection of schema drift. * **Comprehensive Model and Enum Imports**: Enhanced 'alembic/env.py' to ensure all 20 SQLAlchemy model classes and their associated enums are correctly imported, significantly improving 'alembic autogenerate' accuracy and preventing undetected schema changes. * **Schema Reconciliation for shop_products and user_gamification_profiles**: Created a new migration ('20260320_000001_fix_shop_products_schema.py') to reconcile the 'shop_products' table schema with the 'ShopProduct' model and resolve a duplicate index collision on 'user_gamification_profiles', ensuring consistent database structure. * **PostgreSQL Enum Synchronization**: Developed a new migration ('20260320_000002_fix_transactiontype_enum.py') to synchronize the PostgreSQL 'transactiontype' enum with the Python 'TransactionType' enum, including a transaction-safe COMMIT pattern to prevent errors during enum value additions. * **Improved Dialect Compatibility and Idempotency**: Refactored several existing migrations ('seed_gamification_data.py', 'add_pending_request_column.py', 'fix_reaction_one_per_content.py', 'fix_reaction_unique_constraint_add_emoji.py') to improve dialect compatibility, ensure idempotency, and correctly handle index creation across SQLite and PostgreSQL. * **VIPSubscriber Model Alignment**: Updated the 'VIPSubscriber' model to include the 'last_kick_notification_sent_at' column, aligning the model definition with existing database schema changes and preventing runtime AttributeError issues. * **Updated Planning and Research Documentation**: Updated planning documents ('ROADMAP.md', 'STATE.md', and new phase-specific summaries and research documents) to reflect the comprehensive migration correction efforts undertaken in Phase 28, providing clear progress tracking and rationale. 🧠 **New Feature in Public Preview:** You can now enable **Memory** to help **Gemini Code Assist** learn from your team's feedback. This makes future code reviews more consistent and personalized to your project's style. **Click [here](https://codeassist.google/code-review/login) to enable Memory in your admin console.**
Using Gemini Code Assist
The full guide for Gemini Code Assist can be found on our [documentation page](https://developers.google.com/gemini-code-assist/docs/review-github-code), here are some quick tips. Invoking Gemini You can request assistance from Gemini at any point by creating a comment using either `/gemini ` or `@gemini-code-assist `. Below is a summary of the supported commands on the current page. Feature | Command | Description --- | --- | --- Code Review | `/gemini review` | Performs a code review for the current pull request in its current state. Pull Request Summary | `/gemini summary` | Provides a summary of the current pull request in its current state. Comment | @gemini-code-assist | Responds in comments when explicitly tagged, both in pull request comments and review comments. Help | `/gemini help` | Displays a list of available commands. Customization To customize Gemini Code Assist for GitHub experience, repository maintainers can create a configuration file and/or provide a custom code review style guide (such as PEP-8 for Python) by creating and adding files to a `.gemini/` folder in the base of the repository. Detailed instructions can be found [here](https://developers.google.com/gemini-code-assist/docs/customize-gemini-behavior-github). Limitations & Feedback Gemini Code Assist may make mistakes. Please leave feedback on any instances where its feedback is incorrect or counter productive. You can react with :thumbsup: and :thumbsdown: on @gemini-code-assist comments. If you're interested in giving your feedback about your experience with Gemini Code Assist for GitHub and other Google products, sign up [here](https://google.qualtrics.com/jfe/form/SV_2cyuGuTWsEw84yG).
[^1]: Review the [Privacy Notices](https://policies.google.com/privacy), [Generative AI Prohibited Use Policy](https://policies.google.com/terms/generative-ai/use-policy), [Terms of Service](https://policies.google.com/terms), and learn how to configure Gemini Code Assist in GitHub [here](https://developers.google.com/gemini-code-assist/docs/customize-gemini-behavior-github). Gemini can make mistakes, so double check it and [use code with caution](https://support.google.com/legal/answer/13505487).

performed_via_github_app.name: Gemini Code Assist
performed_via_github_app.description:
**Accelerate code reviews and improve code quality**

Bring the power of [Gemini](https://gemini.google.com/) directly to GitHub, providing AI-powered code reviews on your pull requests, with automatic pull request summaries, ready-to-commit code suggestions and the ability to invoke Gemini for assistance at any point on the PR.
